reason: Same root cause as marianatek-required-flag-bloat, but extended across the remaining 18 create commands the generator produced. After the production booking test surfaced the over-required pattern on `me reservations-create`, an audit of the other POST endpoints found the same spec-derived require-storm — totalling ~2300 lines of false-positive required-flag checks. Verified with a sample call: `me credit-cards-create` with no args now returns the API's actual 422 ("number is required, usage_location is required") instead of the spec's invented requirements.
change: Replaced each `if !stdinBody { ... 100s of required-flag checks ... }` block with an empty PATCH-commented block that lets the API surface real-required fields via 422 errors. ~2300 lines removed. `me reservations-create` keeps its bespoke 3-rule validator (different patch above).
